news.title: Study: 40% of Young Bodybuilders Show Silent Heart Disease From Steroid Use
news.title_ko: 스테로이드 쓴 청년 보디빌더 40%에 심장병이 숨어있다
news.summary: A landmark 11-year Danish follow-up study published in Circulation (2025) tracked 1,189 anabolic steroid users vs 59,450 controls and found cardiomyopathy rates nine times higher in AAS users. Arrythmia was the most prevalent cardiovascular outcome. Venous thromboembolism and structural heart changes persisted even years after cessation. Researchers warn that subclinical cardiac damage may be present long before symptoms appear.

news.title: Steroid Users Have 9x Higher Cardiomyopathy Risk — New 11-Year Data
news.title_ko: 스테로이드 유저, 심근병증 위험 9배 — 11년 추적 연구 발표
news.summary: The most comprehensive long-term AAS cardiovascular study to date (Circulation, Feb 2025) reveals that steroid users face a nine-fold elevated risk of cardiomyopathy compared to matched controls. Arrhythmia, thromboembolism, and coronary disease rates were all significantly elevated. The study followed both active and former users, finding that stopping steroids does not fully reverse cardiac remodeling. These findings suggest the bodybuilding community is facing a silent cardiovascular epidemic.

news.title: DNP: The 'Fat-Burning' Drug That Literally Cooks You From the Inside
news.title_ko: DNP: 내부에서 몸을 태워버리는 '지방연소 약물'의 진실
news.summary: 2,4-Dinitrophenol (DNP) causes catastrophic mitochondrial uncoupling, raising core body temperature to a fatal 44°C by converting all cellular energy into heat. It delivers 30-40% metabolic rate boosts but has killed dozens of bodybuilders worldwide — and there is no antidote. Deaths are sudden and often occur at doses only marginally above 'effective' doses. Despite being banned since the 1930s, it remains freely available online under dozens of disguised names.

news.title: The Liver King Effect: Netflix Doc Exposes $11K/Month Steroid Budget Behind 'Ancestral' Brand
news.title_ko: 리버킹 실체: 조상 생활 팔면서 월 1,600만원 스테로이드 쓴 남자
news.summary: Leaked emails and a Netflix documentary confirmed Liver King (Brian Johnson) spent over $11,000 per month on anabolic steroids while publicly evangelizing a 'hormone-free ancestral lifestyle.' The case has become a defining symbol of social media fitness fraud, exposing how supplement empires can be built on systematic deception. Influencers in 2025 and 2026 continue the same pattern — promoting ice baths and breathwork while concealing what they inject. The scandal accelerated public skepticism about any extreme physique claim.

news.title: Instagram's Most Famous 'Natural' Bodybuilder Admits to 10-Year Steroid Use
news.title_ko: '천연 보디빌더' 인스타 스타, 10년간 스테로이드 사용 고백
news.summary: The fitness influencer ecosystem faces a credibility collapse as multiple high-profile 'natural' athletes have admitted steroid use in 2025, following the Liver King template. Research shows fake natural bodybuilders cluster around an FFMI above 25 — a threshold virtually impossible without exogenous hormones. Followers who modeled their programs on these athletes report wasted years chasing unattainable drug-enhanced standards. The NOGEAR standard is clear: if you're lying about what you use, you're selling a lie.

news.title: SARMs Sold as 'Safe Alternatives' Are Crashing Testosterone Levels in Young Men
news.title_ko: SARMs '안전한 대안'이라더니 — 청년들 테스토스테론 폭락 중
news.summary: A 2025 systematic review of nine clinical trials involving 970 patients found that SARMs cause significant hormonal suppression even at low doses. In Ligandrol (LGD-4033) trials, FSH and free testosterone dropped sharply — and while levels returned after discontinuation, recreational users often dose far higher and longer than clinical protocols. The selectivity between muscle activity and LH suppression remains a fundamental limitation that marketing materials omit entirely. Young men are purchasing these compounds with no post-cycle therapy or medical supervision.

news.title: Steroids Triple Heart Attack Risk — Danish Population Study Is the Definitive Proof
news.title_ko: 스테로이드, 심근경색 위험 3배 — 덴마크 대규모 연구 결론
news.summary: The Medscape-reported Danish study of over 60,000 participants is the largest population-level analysis of AAS cardiovascular risk to date. Steroid users showed triple the incidence of myocardial infarction, alongside ninefold cardiomyopathy rates and dramatically elevated arrhythmia. The average age of first cardiac event was 10-15 years earlier than matched controls. What the fitness community calls 'blasting and cruising' appears to be systematically destroying the cardiovascular system by the late 30s.

news.title: The Protein Timing Myth Debunked: The Anabolic Window Is Hours, Not Minutes
news.title_ko: 단백질 타이밍 신화 붕괴 — 동화작용 윈도우는 몇 분이 아닌 몇 시간
news.summary: A landmark meta-analysis integrating data from 2025's most comprehensive protein synthesis studies shows the 'anabolic window' is not the 30-minute panic-sprint to a protein shake that bro-science claims. Muscle protein synthesis remains elevated for 3-5 hours post-resistance exercise, and total daily protein intake (1.6-2.0g/kg) matters far more than precise timing. Whey protein benefits are real but the urgency has been massively oversold by supplement companies. You have time to shower.

news.title: 10 Minutes of Exercise Makes Your Brain Work Better Immediately — New Study
news.title_ko: 운동 10분이 뇌를 즉시 업그레이드한다 — 신경과학 새 발견
news.summary: Northeastern University researchers published in late 2025 confirming that just 10 minutes of moderate exercise before cognitive tasks significantly improves executive function — problem-solving, decision-making, and learning speed. The mechanism involves acute dopamine and noradrenaline release in the hippocampus, stimulating memory circuits. The finding has practical implications for students, executives, and anyone making high-stakes decisions: your pre-meeting workout is not vanity, it's cognitive infrastructure.

news.title: Autopsy Data Confirms: Young Steroid Users Are Dying of Heart Attacks in Their 30s
news.title_ko: 부검 데이터 확인 — 스테로이드 사용 젊은이들, 30대에 심장마비로 죽고 있다
news.summary: A 2025 forensic study in Frontiers in Cardiovascular Medicine analyzed autopsy cases of AAS users and documented consistent patterns: left ventricular hypertrophy, coronary thrombosis, dilated cardiomyopathy, and myocardial fibrosis in men under 35. Immunohistochemistry confirmed direct steroid metabolite deposits in cardiac tissue. These deaths are often classified as 'unexplained' or 'sudden cardiac arrest' in gym settings, masking the true epidemiological toll of recreational AAS abuse.

news.title: Supplement Companies Are Selling You Steroids — And Calling Them 'Dietary Supplements'
news.title_ko: 보충제 회사들이 스테로이드를 '식이 보충제'로 팔고 있다
news.summary: A 2025 MDPI Nutrients study analyzing 5,400 anabolic agents on the black market found widespread contamination and mislabeling in products sold as legal supplements. The FDA has repeatedly warned that bodybuilding products marketed as dietary supplements often illegally contain undisclosed steroids or steroid-like substances. With FTC enforcement weakened under the current administration, consumers have virtually no protection. The supplement industry is a $50 billion business built on a foundation of minimal regulatory oversight.

news.title: Resistance Training Literally Makes Your Brain Younger — New Neuroimaging Evidence
news.title_ko: 근력 운동이 뇌를 실제로 젊게 만든다 — 뇌 영상 새 증거
news.summary: Washington Post and multiple 2025 neuroscience studies confirm that people with greater muscle mass consistently show younger brain structures on MRI. Resistance training reduces age-related hippocampal shrinkage, improves white matter integrity, and slows cortical thinning. The brain-body connection is bidirectional: building muscle is simultaneously building cognitive reserve. This is perhaps the most underreported benefit of lifting — you're not just changing your body, you're protecting your mind.
news.source: Washington Post / Frontiers in Human Neuroscience

news.title: Steroids Give You 3x The Muscle Gains — And 9x The Heart Disease Risk
news.title_ko: 스테로이드는 근육을 3배 키우고 심장병 위험을 9배 높인다
news.summary: A direct comparison of 100-day training outcomes shows steroid-assisted lifters gain approximately 13 lbs of muscle vs 3.7 lbs for natural lifters — roughly 3.5x the gains. But the same population studies show 9x higher cardiomyopathy rates and triple the heart attack risk. The tradeoff calculus is stark: you are mortgaging decades of cardiac health for a few extra pounds of muscle per cycle. The FFMI ceiling for natural lifters (25) is achievable through hard training alone.

news.title: Exercise Triggers New Brain Cell Growth — Scientists Finally Found the Mechanism
news.title_ko: 운동이 새 뇌세포를 만드는 메커니즘 — 과학자들 드디어 밝혔다
news.summary: A breakthrough study published in PsyPost (2025) identified the precise molecular mechanism by which exercise induces neurogenesis in the hippocampus. Extracellular vesicles released during exercise carry specific signaling proteins that trigger neural stem cell proliferation. When these vesicles were injected into sedentary mice, the sedentary mice grew new neurons. The finding could unlock pharmaceutical approaches to depression and Alzheimer's treatment based on exercise biochemistry.

news.title: WADA's 2026 Prohibited List Is Here — New Substances, New Loopholes
news.title_ko: WADA 2026 금지 약물 목록 발표 — 새 물질, 새 허점
news.summary: The World Anti-Doping Agency's 2026 prohibited list, effective from January 1, arrived ahead of the Milano Cortina Winter Games with key additions to anabolic agents, peptide hormones, and metabolic modulators. Meanwhile, the US withheld $7.3 million in WADA funding over unresolved controversies including the Chinese swimmer doping scandal. NCAA anti-doping gaps were exposed by a March 2026 investigation into an Iowa State runner allegedly admitting to banned substance use. Clean sport governance has never been more contested.

news.title: AAS Causes Myocardial Fibrosis That Never Goes Away — Even After You Quit
news.title_ko: 스테로이드로 인한 심근 섬유화, 끊어도 사라지지 않는다
news.summary: New molecular research in MDPI's International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2025) maps the precise mechanisms of AAS-induced cardiac remodeling: dyslipidemia, endothelial dysfunction, prothrombotic cascades, myocardial fibrosis, and arrhythmogenesis. Crucially, cardiac MRI studies show that myocardial fibrosis — scar tissue in the heart muscle — persists in former users even after years of abstinence. The heart does not fully heal. Every cycle adds to a permanent deficit.

news.title: Creatine Monohydrate: The One Supplement With Actual Proof Behind It
news.title_ko: 크레아틴 모노하이드레이트 — 실제 증거가 있는 유일한 보충제
news.summary: While the supplement industry floods the market with unproven products, a 2025 meta-analysis confirms creatine monohydrate (3-5g/day) consistently increases muscle thickness and cross-sectional area in interventions lasting 8-12 weeks or more, mediated by enhanced training volume and intramuscular ATP regeneration. No loading phase is required. No exotic forms are superior to basic monohydrate. In an industry built on marketing, creatine is the rare exception where the science actually matches the claim.

news.title: FTC Enforcement on Fake Supplements Collapses Under New Administration
news.title_ko: FTC 허위 보충제 단속 붕괴 — 소비자 보호 사실상 실종
news.summary: The FTC under the Trump administration has not filed a single new enforcement action against supplement makers, and dropped a 2021 lawsuit against a company marketing saline as a COVID-19 cure. This represents a dramatic reversal from Biden-era enforcement that required scientific substantiation for health claims. Legion Media LLC and Golden Sunrise Nutraceutical faced action in late 2025, but the pipeline has dried up since. The $50 billion supplement industry now operates in a near-regulatory vacuum.

news.title: Natural Lifters Hit Genetic Ceiling at FFMI 25 — Here's What That Means For You
news.title_ko: 자연인의 유전적 상한선 FFMI 25 — 당신에게 의미하는 것
news.summary: McLean Hospital research established that no verified drug-free athlete exceeds an FFMI (fat-free mass index) of 25, while steroid users averaged 24.8. This means the men with 'unbelievable naturals' on social media with FFMIs of 27-30 are almost certainly using PEDs. Understanding this threshold is liberating: it reframes 'natural failure' as success within biological reality. Building to an FFMI of 22-24 through natural training represents an extraordinary physique — and it can be done.

news.title: Depression Lifts With Resistance Training — The Mechanism Is Now Understood
news.title_ko: 우울증, 근력 운동으로 완화된다 — 메커니즘 규명
news.summary: A 2025 Frontiers in Psychiatry meta-analysis confirms that aerobic exercise reduces anxiety and resistance exercise specifically alleviates depression, with distinct neurobiological pathways. Resistance training elevates BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor), reduces cortisol chronically, and improves sleep architecture — a triple mechanism that pharmacological antidepressants partially replicate. For mild-to-moderate depression, the effect size of resistance training is comparable to SSRIs with zero side effects.

news.title: Protein Above 2.0g/kg Has No Extra Muscle-Building Benefit — New Meta-Analysis
news.title_ko: 단백질 2.0g/kg 초과 섭취, 추가 근육 합성 효과 없다 — 메타분석
news.summary: A major 2025 meta-analysis synthesizing data from Examine.com and multiple RCTs confirms that protein's muscle-building benefits plateau around 1.6-2.0g per kg of bodyweight daily. Consuming 3g/kg or 4g/kg — popular in some bodybuilding protocols — provides zero additional anabolic stimulus while adding unnecessary caloric load and potential renal strain. Companies selling high-protein products at premium prices are exploiting a dosing myth that the science has definitively buried.

news.title: Compression Gear Review: It Helps Recovery — But Won't Make You Faster
news.title_ko: 압박 의류 팩트체크 — 회복에는 효과, 퍼포먼스엔 미미
news.summary: An updated 2025 systematic review and meta-analysis of 51 studies (899 participants) published in the Journal of Sport and Health Science found compression garments provide no significant improvement in race time or time to exhaustion. However, compression gear does measurably reduce muscle soreness, decrease soft-tissue vibration, improve venous return, and reduce blood lactate post-exercise. The verdict: wear it during recovery, not for a PR. The performance marketing claims are largely unsupported.
news.source: Journal of Sport and Health Science / ScienceDirect

news.title: Aerobic Exercise vs Resistance Training for Brain Health: New Winner Declared
news.title_ko: 유산소 vs 근력 — 뇌 건강에 누가 더 좋나? 2025년 판정 나왔다
news.summary: A comprehensive 2025 review in Frontiers in Human Neuroscience establishes that both modalities improve brain health via distinct mechanisms. Aerobic exercise excels at hippocampal neurogenesis and anxiety reduction via elevated BDNF and serotonin. Resistance training uniquely improves executive function, reduces depression, and creates structural brain changes correlating with muscle mass. The optimal protocol combines both — but if forced to choose, resistance training delivers broader psychiatric benefit.

news.title: SARMs Are Not Approved for Human Use — And the FDA Is Warning Gyms
news.title_ko: SARMs은 인체 사용 미승인 — FDA가 헬스장에 직접 경고 나섰다
news.summary: Despite widespread recreational use, no SARM has received FDA approval for any human indication. The FDA has issued multiple warnings targeting supplement retailers and gyms selling SARM products, classifying them as unapproved new drugs. Clinical trials show SARMs cause hormonal suppression, potential hepatotoxicity, and unknown long-term risks. The 'research chemical' legal gray area is being actively closed. Recreational users are conducting unsupervised experiments on their own endocrine systems.

news.title: Why Steroid-Induced Psychiatric Damage Can Last Decades After Quitting
news.title_ko: 스테로이드 끊어도 수십 년간 정신 건강 손상 — 연구 경고
news.summary: Long-term research from Oslo University Hospital (OUS) documents persistent psychiatric and cognitive consequences of AAS abuse: depression, hypomania, cognitive impairment, and dependence patterns lasting 10-20 years after cessation in heavy users. The brain's androgen receptor density is permanently altered by prolonged supraphysiological hormone exposure. Stopping steroids often triggers a severe depressive crash — the very mechanism that drives relapse and continued use. This is a public health crisis hiding in plain sight.

news.title: High Volume Training Is Not Overtraining: New biorxiv Data Resets the Rules
news.title_ko: 고볼륨 훈련은 오버트레이닝이 아니다 — biorxiv 새 데이터
news.summary: A February 2026 preprint from biorxiv found that large increases in resistance training volume — well above typical recommendations — do not impair skeletal muscle protein synthesis or hypertrophic signaling. Participants performing 40+ sets per muscle group per week showed no signs of physiological overtraining, challenging decade-old volume ceiling dogma. Recovery capacity appears far more individual than previously assumed. The study could fundamentally reshape programming guidelines for advanced lifters.

news.title: The Whey Protein Market Is Worth $12B — Most Products Don't Justify the Premium
news.title_ko: 웨이 프로틴 시장 12조원 — 대부분 프리미엄 가격 정당화 못해
news.summary: A 2025 scientometric analysis of 33 years of protein supplementation research concludes that basic whey protein from commodity sources is functionally identical to premium 'performance' formulations for muscle protein synthesis. Leucine content is the primary driver of anabolic signaling — not brand, filtration method, or added ingredients. The massive price differentials between grocery-brand whey and influencer-promoted 'superior' proteins are built on marketing, not science.
news.source: Journal of Health, Population and Nutrition / Springer

news.title: Ethiopian Runner Gets 5-Year Doping Ban for IV Infusion — The Grey Area Exposed
news.title_ko: 에티오피아 육상 선수, 정맥주사로 5년 도핑 금지 — 규정의 회색지대
news.summary: USADA banned 22-year-old Ethiopian runner Adane Anmaw Mengesha for five years in March 2026 for receiving IV infusions exceeding WADA's 100mL/12hr limit — even though the treatment was medically prescribed. The case exposes how anti-doping rules collide with legitimate medical care, and how athletes in developing nations often lack the medical infrastructure to meet exception procedures. Meanwhile, the NCAA's anti-doping gaps remain unaddressed following the Iowa State investigation.

news.title: Female Athletes Build Muscle Just as Well as Men — But MPS Signals Are Lower
news.title_ko: 여성 운동선수도 남성만큼 근육 발달 가능 — 단, 단백질 합성 신호는 더 낮아
news.summary: A 2025 ACSM study found that hypertrophy at the fiber and whole-muscle level was statistically identical between trained males and females, despite males showing 23% higher exercise-induced myofibrillar protein synthesis signals. The practical implication: women should train with the same intensity and volume as men, but MPS signaling differences may explain why some women respond better to more frequent training sessions. Equal effort. Equal results. Different physiology, same potential.

news.title: GH Gut, Palumboism, and the Hidden Cost of Growth Hormone Abuse in Bodybuilding
news.title_ko: GH 배, 팔럼보이즘 — 성장호르몬 남용의 숨겨진 대가
news.summary: The distended 'growth hormone gut' or Palumboism seen in modern mass-monster bodybuilding is caused by chronic Human Growth Hormone and insulin abuse causing visceral organ hypertrophy — literally enlarging the liver, intestines, and heart. This is not a side effect that resolves post-cycle. Modern open-class bodybuilding is producing competitors whose internal organs are so enlarged they cannot safely reduce caloric intake. The aesthetic standard has become physiologically incompatible with survival.

news.title: Bogus Celebrity Supplement Endorsements: FTC Targets $409K in Consumer Fraud Refunds
news.title_ko: 가짜 유명인 보충제 보증 사기 — FTC, 소비자에 6억원 환불 추진
news.summary: The FTC announced over $409,000 in refunds to consumers defrauded by weight-loss supplement marketer Roca Labs, with 34 consumer claims filed. Separately, the agency cracked down on supplement marketers using fabricated celebrity endorsements to drive sales. These cases represent a fraction of the industry's deceptive practices — the total estimated consumer losses from false supplement claims run into the billions annually. Most victims never see a refund.

news.title: Sleep Is Your Most Powerful Legal Performance-Enhancing Drug
news.title_ko: 수면은 당신이 사용 가능한 가장 강력한 합법적 퍼포먼스 향상제
news.summary: A convergence of 2025 sleep and performance research confirms that 8+ hours of sleep increases muscle protein synthesis rates, elevates natural testosterone by up to 74%, optimizes GH secretion, reduces cortisol, and enhances reaction time and cognitive performance. No supplement stack replicates this. Sleep deprivation below 6 hours negates a significant portion of training adaptations. The most underutilized performance edge in fitness culture costs nothing.
news.source: Journal of Sleep Research / Multiple meta-analyses

news.title: DNP Is Still Being Sold Online Under 26 Different Names — FDA Cannot Stop It
news.title_ko: DNP, 아직도 온라인에서 26개 이명으로 팔린다 — FDA도 막지 못해
news.summary: Despite a ban dating to the 1930s and at least 50 documented worldwide deaths between 2010-2020, 2,4-dinitrophenol remains freely available online under names including Dinosan, Dnoc, Solfo Black, Nitrophen, Aldifen, and Chemox. Internet forums continue to share 'dosing guides' despite an 11.9% fatality rate in reported overdose cases. No antidote exists. Bodybuilding communities that normalize its use are directly complicit in preventable deaths. FXXK FAKES. STAY NATURAL.

news.title: How Bodybuilders' Black Market Steroid Labs Are Poisoning Users With Counterfeit Compounds
news.title_ko: 보디빌더 블랙마켓 스테로이드 랩 — 가짜 화합물로 사용자 독살 중
news.summary: A large-scale analysis of 5,400 black market anabolic agents found widespread mislabeling, contamination, and outright fake compounds. Many products contain no active steroid, incorrect doses, bacterial contamination, or entirely different and more dangerous substances. Users injecting these compounds have developed abscesses, sepsis, and permanent injection site damage. The black market steroid industry is a criminal enterprise with zero quality control, exploiting a community's desperation.

news.title: 2-Year Lifestyle Intervention Study: Diet + Exercise Makes Your Brain 1-2 Years Younger
news.title_ko: 2년 생활습관 개선, 뇌를 1~2년 젊게 만든다 — 대규모 임상 결과
news.summary: Participants in a comprehensive 2-year lifestyle intervention combining aerobic exercise, Mediterranean diet, blood pressure management, and cognitive training scored significantly better on standardized cognitive tests than controls — with brain ages estimated 1-2 years younger on functional assessments. NPR's 2025 neuroscience year-in-review cited this as a defining study of the decade. The finding has profound implications for preventive medicine: aging is not linear, and lifestyle choices measurably alter its trajectory.

news.title: Pre-Workout Products: Caffeine Works, Everything Else Is Noise
news.title_ko: 프리워크아웃 제품 팩트체크 — 카페인은 효과 있고 나머지는 마케팅
news.summary: A systematic review of pre-workout supplement ingredients in 2025 confirmed that caffeine (3-6mg/kg) is the only ingredient with robust, replicated evidence for acute performance enhancement. Beta-alanine provides modest endurance benefits at effective doses but causes uncomfortable paresthesia. Citrulline malate shows marginal vasodilation effects. Proprietary blends hiding ingredient doses, exotic adaptogens, and 'focus matrix' formulations are marketing vehicles with no meaningful clinical evidence.

news.title: Steroid Use Causes Severe Kidney Failure — Long-Term Data That Gets Ignored
news.title_ko: 스테로이드 장기 사용, 심각한 신장 손상 초래 — 무시되는 데이터
news.summary: NIDA and multiple longitudinal studies confirm that chronic AAS use leads to focal segmental glomerulosclerosis (FSGS) — a form of kidney scarring that can progress to end-stage renal disease requiring dialysis or transplant. The mechanism involves chronic hypertension, prothrombotic changes, and direct androgenic toxicity to podocytes. Unlike cardiac damage which produces symptoms like breathlessness, renal damage is silent until function drops below 30%. Many steroid users in their 40s and 50s are discovering their kidneys are irreversibly compromised.

news.title: The Science of Rest-Pause Training: Why Short Rests Unlock More Hypertrophy
news.title_ko: 레스트-포즈 훈련의 과학 — 짧은 휴식이 더 많은 비대를 만드는 이유
news.summary: Emerging 2025 hypertrophy research clarifies the mechanisms behind rest-pause and cluster set protocols. Short intra-set rest periods (10-30 seconds) allow near-maximal motor unit recruitment across more total repetitions, accumulating greater mechanical tension and metabolic stress per unit time than traditional straight sets. For time-constrained lifters, the hypertrophic equivalent of a 60-minute session can be achieved in approximately 35-40 minutes using rest-pause methodology, per the latest biorxiv preprint data.
news.source: biorxiv / Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research

news.title: Compression Gear Reduces Post-Exercise Cardiac Strain — But the Effect Is Tiny
news.title_ko: 압박 의류, 운동 후 심장 부담 줄인다 — 단, 효과는 미미
news.summary: A 2025 randomized crossover trial on compression pants found that post-exercise wearing significantly increased stroke volume and cardiac output while reducing heart rate and blood lactate concentration. However, the effect sizes are small and largely imperceptible in trained athletes. The studies used precise pressure levels (18-22 mmHg) rarely matched by commercial products. Most compression apparel sold at premium prices delivers far less physiological benefit than the clinical research conditions suggest.
news.source: PMC / MDPI Journal of Functional Morphology

news.title: Mindfulness Training Combined With Exercise Reduces Cortisol Better Than Either Alone
news.title_ko: 마음챙김 훈련 + 운동, 각각보다 코르티솔 감소 효과 더 높아
news.summary: A 2025 intervention study combining mindfulness-based stress reduction (MBSR) with structured resistance training found synergistic cortisol-lowering effects exceeding either modality alone. Chronic cortisol elevation is a primary driver of muscle catabolism, fat accumulation, impaired recovery, and depression. Athletes who dismiss mental training as 'soft' are leaving a measurable physiological advantage on the table. The mind-body split in sports science is increasingly unsupported by data.
news.source: Frontiers in Psychiatry / Journal of Behavioral Medicine

news.title: The Mediterranean Diet + Exercise Combo Is the Most Evidence-Backed Anti-Aging Stack
news.title_ko: 지중해식 식단 + 운동 조합 — 가장 증거 많은 안티에이징 스택
news.summary: Converging 2025 data from the FINGER trial and related lifestyle intervention studies positions the Mediterranean diet combined with structured exercise as the most robustly validated anti-aging intervention available. The combination reduces inflammatory markers, preserves telomere length, maintains cognitive function, and reduces all-cause mortality more effectively than any supplement or pharmaceutical currently available to healthy adults. The irony: this 'stack' is accessible and affordable. It simply requires consistency.

news.title: Leucine Is the True Anabolic Trigger — Not Total Protein Quantity
news.title_ko: 류신이 진짜 동화작용 트리거 — 총 단백질 양이 아니라
news.summary: 2025 protein research from McMaster University and multiple meta-analyses confirms that leucine content per meal, not total protein grams, is the primary activator of mTOR and downstream muscle protein synthesis. A minimum of 2-3g of leucine per meal is required to maximally stimulate MPS. This explains why low-leucine protein sources (gelatin, collagen, many plant proteins) fail to optimally stimulate muscle growth regardless of total protein content. Strategic leucine targeting is more important than hitting an arbitrary daily gram target.

news.title: Best Practice Guidelines for AAS Users in Primary Care: Delphi Consensus Coming 2026
news.title_ko: 1차 의료에서 AAS 사용자 가이드라인 — 2026 델파이 합의안 발표 예정
news.summary: A modified Delphi consensus study protocol published in JMIR Research Protocols (2025) is developing the first formal best-practice guidelines for managing male recreational AAS users in primary care settings. The protocol acknowledges the gap between harm reduction needs and current medical practice, where most users receive no medical monitoring. Expected publication in summer 2026, the guidelines could transform how physicians approach a rapidly growing population of steroid users seeking medical care without judgment.

news.title: Qualitative Study: Why AAS Users Keep Using Despite Knowing the Risks
news.title_ko: 정성 연구 — AAS 사용자들이 위험을 알면서도 계속 사용하는 이유
news.summary: A 2025 PMC qualitative study documenting risk management behaviors among recreational steroid users found that most users are well-informed about health risks but employ motivated reasoning, risk minimization, and community normalization to continue use. Identity fusion with the 'enhanced lifestyle' community, sunk cost psychology, and rapid aesthetic results create powerful retention mechanisms. Harm reduction is most effective when it meets users where they are rather than leading with prohibition framing.
news.source: PMC / Managing Risks in AAS Use Study

news.title: Optical Coherence Tomography Reveals Coronary Plaque in Steroid-Using Athletes
news.title_ko: 광간섭단층촬영으로 스테로이드 사용 선수 관상동맥 플라크 확인
news.summary: A cutting-edge JACC case report series using optical coherence tomography (OCT) — the highest resolution coronary imaging available — documented atherosclerotic plaque formation and erosion in anabolic steroid users presenting with acute coronary syndrome. Some patients were in their late 20s with no traditional cardiovascular risk factors other than AAS use. OCT imaging revealed lipid-rich plaques with thin fibrous caps — the morphology most prone to acute rupture and fatal MI.

news.title: 33 Years of Protein Supplementation Research: What Scientometrics Reveals
news.title_ko: 33년 단백질 보충 연구 총정리 — 사이언토메트릭스가 밝힌 진실
news.summary: A comprehensive scientometric analysis spanning 1992-2025 and published in the Journal of Health, Population and Nutrition analyzed 33 years of protein supplementation literature in the context of resistance training. McMaster University led with 37 publications and 3,526 citations. Key finding: the field has repeatedly confirmed diminishing returns above 1.6g/kg/day, the primacy of leucine, and the equivalency of most protein sources when matched for leucine content. The science is settled — the industry ignores it.
news.source: Journal of Health, Population and Nutrition / Springer
